title: The Ventotene Manifesto
subtitle: For a Free and United Europe
body: Written in secret on the island of Ventotene, where Spinelli and Rossi were detained under Fascist confinement, the manifesto argued that the nation-state system had become the root cause of European wars and could only be transcended through a federal union. It circulated clandestinely through the Italian resistance and became the founding document of the European federalist movement.

citations.author: Altiero Spinelli and Ernesto Rossi
citations.title: For a Free and United Europe: A Draft Manifesto

title: The Schuman Declaration
subtitle: A proposal for a European Coal and Steel Community
body: French Foreign Minister Robert Schuman, drawing on a text drafted by Jean Monnet, proposed pooling European coal and steel production under a common High Authority — making war between France and Germany 'not merely unthinkable, but materially impossible.' The declaration led directly to the Treaty of Paris (1951) establishing the ECSC, the first supranational European institution. 9 May is now marked annually as Europe Day.

citations.author: Robert Schuman
citations.title: Declaration of 9 May 1950
citations.publisher: French Ministry of Foreign Affairs / European Commission

title: Treaty of Rome
subtitle: Establishing the European Economic Community
body: Signed by Belgium, France, Italy, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, and West Germany, the Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community (EEC), creating a common market and customs union. It committed signatories to 'an ever closer union among the peoples of Europe' — a phrase that has anchored federalist interpretation of European law ever since, and which was formally removed for the United Kingdom's special settlement in 2016.

citations.author: European Communities
citations.title: Treaty Establishing the European Economic Community

title: Spinelli Draft Treaty on European Union
subtitle: The European Parliament's federal proposal
body: Altiero Spinelli, by then a member of the European Parliament, led the Crocodile Club — an informal grouping of MEPs — to draft a Treaty on European Union that went substantially further than any existing Community text in the direction of a federal constitution. The Parliament adopted the draft on 14 February 1984 by 237 votes to 31. Though never ratified by member states, it directly shaped the Single European Act (1986) and the Maastricht Treaty (1992).
primarySourceLabel: CVCE — Draft Treaty on European Union (1984)

citations.author: European Parliament
citations.title: Draft Treaty Establishing the European Union

title: Treaty of Maastricht
subtitle: Establishing the European Union
body: The Treaty on European Union, signed at Maastricht, created the European Union as a political entity, introduced EU citizenship, established the framework for Economic and Monetary Union (leading to the euro), and extended qualified majority voting in the Council. It represented the most significant integration step since the Treaty of Rome. The Danish electorate initially rejected it in a 1992 referendum (50.7% against) before approving it in 1993 after the Edinburgh Agreement secured four opt-outs.

title: Treaty of Lisbon
subtitle: Amending the Treaty on European Union and the Treaty establishing the European Community
body: Signed following the failed Constitutional Treaty (rejected in French and Dutch referendums in 2005), the Lisbon Treaty gave the EU its current legal and institutional structure. It created the post of President of the European Council (filled by permanent election rather than rotating presidence), strengthened the European Parliament's co-legislative role, made the Charter of Fundamental Rights legally binding, and introduced the Article 50 withdrawal mechanism. It entered into force on 1 December 2009.

title: Spinelli Group Founded
subtitle: Cross-party MEP alliance for deeper EU integration
body: Named after Altiero Spinelli, the Spinelli Group was founded in September 2010 by MEPs from multiple political families (Liberal, Green, Social Democrat) committed to the goal of a federal European Union. Its founding members included Guy Verhofstadt (ALDE), Andrew Duff (ALDE), and Daniel Cohn-Bendit (Greens). The group subsequently published 'A Fundamental Law of the European Union' (2013) as a worked constitutional proposal.

citations.author: Guy Verhofstadt, Andrew Duff, Daniel Cohn-Bendit et al.
citations.title: A Fundamental Law of the European Union

title: Conference on the Future of Europe — Final Report
subtitle: Citizen assemblies propose treaty change
body: The Conference on the Future of Europe — a year-long participatory exercise involving citizen panels, national parliaments, the European Parliament, Council, and Commission — delivered its final report on 9 May 2022. It contained 49 proposals and 326 measures, with many citizen panels explicitly recommending treaty change to strengthen EU competences in health, climate, and foreign policy. The European Parliament voted in June 2022 to convene an Article 48 Convention; the Council's subsequent inaction became itself a point of democratic controversy.

citations.author: Conference on the Future of Europe
citations.title: Final Report
